notes: Shared 50% physical custody adjustment (SPCA) applies 150% multiplier when parents share equal custody; no defined threshold below 50% for graduated adjustment
Shared custody applies when each parent has 30%+ overnights (110/yr); each parent's obligation calculated and offset based on income shares and time percentages
Parenting time table uses guideline days (12+ hrs = 1 day); adjustment percentage scales from 0 to 50% at 164+ days; no minimum threshold to begin receiving credit
Court may adjust support when payor parent has 141+ overnights; completion of separate worksheet required; adjustment amount at court's discretion
statute_citation: Ark. Admin. Order No. 10 Section V

notes: Timeshare formula CS=K[HN-(H%)(TN)] integrates parenting time percentage directly; no threshold required; H% = percentage of time with higher earner
2026 changes removed 93-overnight threshold; new parenting time table starts at 0 overnights (0% credit) scaling to 50% at 182.5 overnights; nonlinear curve
Shared custody found when parenting time substantially exceeds standard visitation; no fixed overnight count; court applies cross-credit calculation at approximately equal time
Graduated credits: 0-79 overnights = 0%; 80-124 = 10% credit; 125-163 = 30% credit; 164+ overnights triggers joint custody calculation using Melson formula
statute_citation: 13 Del.C. 514; Del. Fam. Ct. R. Civ. P. 52(c)

notes: Substantial time-sharing at 20%+ overnights (73/yr); basic obligation multiplied by 1.5 then cross-credited based on each parent's overnight percentage and income share
Mandatory parenting time adjustment effective Jan 1 2026 using nonlinear x^2.5 formula; court-ordered days raised to power of 2.5 for each parent; replaces discretionary deviation
Extensive time-sharing worksheet required when either parent has 143+ overnights (39%+) or parents share equal time; obligation multiplied by 1.5 and cross-credited
statute_citation: HRS 576D-7; Hawaii Child Support Guidelines Section VII

notes: Shared custody applies when each parent has 25%+ overnights (91/yr); separate shared custody worksheet used; obligation divided proportionally by income and time
Shared parenting when each parent has 146+ overnights (40%); basic obligation multiplied by 1.5; each parent's share calculated by income proportion and time percentage
Parenting Time Table (PT) provides graduated credit starting at 52 overnights; credit increases nonlinearly as overnights increase toward 182.5; proportionally larger increases at higher levels
statute_citation: Ind. Child Support Guideline 6; Ind. R. Trial P. 33

notes: Extraordinary visitation credit when noncustodial parent has 127+ court-ordered overnights per year; credit applied to noncustodial parent's share of basic obligation
Graduated reduction: 35-39% time = 5% reduction; 40-44% = 10%; 45-49% = 15%; extended parenting time adjustment for 14+ consecutive days up to 50% monthly reduction
statute_citation: K.S.A. 38-2277; Kansas Child Support Guidelines Section IV.D

notes: Shared parenting time credit requires minimum 73 days/yr (day = 12+ consecutive hours); graduated scale: 73-87 days to 182.5 days with increasing percentages from ~10% to 50%
Joint custody credit available when paying parent has 73+ days; shared custody (approximately equal time) uses 1.5x multiplier with cross-credit; day = minimum 4 hours
Substantially equal care required for shared custody adjustment; enhanced support entitlement calculated when parents share equal care but have unequal incomes; no fixed overnight number
Graduated adjustment: 92-109 overnights (25-30%) uses shared custody adjustment; 110-127 overnights uses worksheet B; 128+ uses full shared physical custody formula
Shared custody adjustment when each parent has 33%+ parenting time; at 50/50 guidelines run twice with each parent as recipient and difference paid; 33-50% range uses interpolation
statute_citation: Mass. Child Support Guidelines Section II.D

notes: Parental time offset adjusts base obligation using overnight count; applied whenever overnights can be determined; 128+ overnights triggers different calculation tier; 21-night change = modification threshold
statute_citation: MCL 552.605; Michigan Child Support Formula Manual 3.03

notes: Parenting expense adjustment uses nonlinear formula based on percentage of parenting time; 2025 changes lowered generous-credit threshold from 45.1% to ~40%; applies from 10%+ parenting time
No statutory formula for parenting time adjustment; percentage-of-income model considers only obligor income; courts may deviate based on shared custody but no fixed threshold or formula
Form 14 overnight adjustment table: 36+ overnights triggers credit starting at 6%; scales through 18 tiers to 34% at 181-183 overnights; below 36 overnights no adjustment
statute_citation: Mo. Sup. Ct. R. 88.01; Form 14 Line 11

notes: Parenting time adjustment when noncustodial parent has 110+ overnights (30%); adjustment multiplier = (1 - 2 x NCP time percentage); day = majority of 24-hour calendar period
109-142 overnights: worksheet 3 use is discretionary; 142+ overnights: rebuttable presumption worksheet 3 applies; joint custody calculation offsets each parent's obligation
statute_citation: Neb. Ct. R. 4-212; Neb. Rev. Stat. 42-364

notes: Joint physical custody when each parent has 40%+ time; each parent's obligation calculated separately under guidelines; higher-income parent pays difference to lower-income parent
No formulaic parenting time adjustment; equal or approximately equal schedule shall not by itself constitute ground for adjustment; court considers specific circumstances case-by-case
Shared parenting at 104+ overnights (28%); parent of alternate residence worksheet (Appendix IX-D) applies; overnight = 12+ hours custodial time; different calculation method than sole custody
statute_citation: N.J. Ct. R. Appendix IX-A; N.J.S.A. 2A:34-23

notes: Shared responsibility when each parent has 35%+ of year (~128 days); Worksheet B used; each parent retains proportion of basic obligation equal to days with child divided by 365

No parenting time formula; CSSA applies regardless of custody arrangement; custodial parent = parent with majority of overnights; court may deviate under paragraph (f) factors but no automatic adjustment
Shared custody when each parent has 123+ overnights (33.7%); combined obligation increased by 50% (1.5x); allocated by income share and cross-multiplied by time percentages
Extended parenting time when paying parent has 100+ overnights per year; adjustment reduces obligation proportionally; percentage-of-income model with parenting time modifier
Automatic 10% reduction at 90+ overnights; court shall consider additional deviation above 90; at 147+ overnights court must grant deviation or explain why not in writing
Presumptive adjustment at 121+ overnights; factor varies: 121-131 nights = factor 2; 132-143 = factor 1.75; 144+ = factor 1.5; adjustment is presumptive but rebuttable
Parenting time credit at 73+ overnights (20%); uses logistic formula for credit percentage based on overnight share; one of most detailed formulas in nation; 2-year average of overnights used
Schedule assumes 30% obligor time; additional 10% reduction in support at 40% parenting time; incremental increases to 20% reduction at 50%; adjustment built into revised schedule base
Shared physical placement at 128+ overnights (35%); income shares cross-calculation adjusts obligation based on time each parent directly supports child; below 128 = sole custody calculation
statute_citation: R.I. Family Court Admin. Order 2014-7; R.I.G.L. 15-5-16.2

notes: Shared custody at 109+ overnights (30%); 109-128 overnights uses graduated formula between Worksheet A and C amounts; 128+ overnights uses full shared custody Worksheet C
statute_citation: S.C. Code Regs. 114-4730; SC Child Support Guidelines

notes: Shared parenting when child resides 180+ nights per year in each home; cross-credit based on nights with each parent; court considers whether credit would substantially harm child's standard of living
Parenting time adjustment begins at 68 days; day = majority of 24-hour period (12+ hours); graduated standard deviation table; separate calculation for children at 50% with each parent

No parenting time formula or automatic adjustment; percentage-of-income model based on obligor net resources; court may consider possession time as deviation factor under 154.123 but no fixed threshold
Joint physical custody at 111+ overnights (30.4%); above 110 overnights: multiply excess nights by 0.0027 by combined obligation for additional credit; table-based adjustment
25-30% parenting time: shared costs table adjustment; 30%+ parenting time: obligation increased by 50% and divided by income proportion and custody time; overnight = physical custody
Shared custody at 90+ days per year; day = 24-hour period; overnight period with <24 hours = half day per parent; shared custody obligation calculated using separate worksheet and offset
No automatic formula; 90+ overnights commonly referenced as benchmark for requesting deviation; parent must specifically request adjustment; court has discretion to grant based on circumstances
Extended shared parenting at 35%+ (127+ overnights); basic obligation multiplied by 1.6; divided by income share; cross-multiplied by other parent's time percentage; offset calculation
Shared placement at 25%+ overnights (92/yr); base obligation reduced proportionally to overnight percentages; higher-obligation parent pays difference; percentage-of-income base model
Shared responsibility when each parent has 25%+ overnights (~92 nights); obligation multiplied by 150%; divided by income proportion; cross-multiplied by other parent's time; offset paid
